description: Iraq invaded Kuwait, removing a major OPEC producer's exports from the market and triggering fears of a wider regional supply disruption.

event_name: Gulf War / Operation Desert Storm begins
description: A US-led coalition launched military action to expel Iraqi forces from Kuwait, raising uncertainty over Gulf oil infrastructure and shipping lanes.
The collapse of the Thai baht triggered a regional financial crisis that sharply reduced Asian oil demand growth expectations.
Facing a price collapse from oversupply and weak Asian demand, OPEC and non-OPEC producers agreed to cut output to support prices.
The attacks on the United States created short-term demand uncertainty and broader macroeconomic and geopolitical risk premia in energy markets.
The invasion raised concerns about disruption to Iraqi oil production and regional supply stability.
Brent prices peaked amid strong global demand, a weak US dollar, and speculative investment flows, shortly before the financial crisis unwound demand.

event_name: Collapse of Lehman Brothers / Global Financial Crisis
description: The onset of the global financial crisis caused a sharp contraction in global demand expectations, driving one of the fastest oil price collapses on record.
Widespread political uprisings across MENA countries introduced sustained supply-risk premia across several oil-producing nations.
Civil conflict in Libya sharply curtailed the country's crude exports, tightening supply of light sweet crude grades.

event_name: OPEC declines to cut production (Vienna meeting)
description: OPEC, led by Saudi Arabia, opted to maintain output despite a growing supply glut, prioritizing market share over price support and accelerating a price collapse.

event_name: Brent crude falls to multi-year low (~$27/barrel)
description: Sustained oversupply, resilient US shale production, and slowing demand growth drove Brent to its lowest level since the early 2000s.
OPEC agreed with Russia and other non-OPEC producers to cut output for the first time since 2008, aiming to rebalance the oversupplied market.

event_name: US reimposes sanctions on Iranian oil exports
description: Renewed US sanctions targeted Iran's crude exports, tightening available global supply, though waivers granted to some importers softened the impact.

event_name: Saudi Arabia-Russia oil price war begins
description: After OPEC+ talks collapsed, Saudi Arabia launched a price war by discounting crude and ramping production, causing the largest one-day price drop since 1991.

event_name: WTI futures turn negative amid COVID-19 demand collapse
description: Collapsing global demand from COVID-19 lockdowns combined with storage capacity constraints briefly pushed US crude futures below zero; Brent also fell to multi-decade lows.
The invasion triggered sanctions on Russian energy exports and heightened fears of a major global supply disruption, sending Brent above $100/barrel.
